name: Teriyaki Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients in a container& mix until sugar dissolves.
Add beef or chicken, cover& refrigerate for at least 3 hours, stirring often.

name: Quick & Easy Chili
directions: Cook beed, onion, and garlic in large skillet until beef is browned.
Drain.
Stir in beans, salsa, chilis, chili powder, oregano, and cumin.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low& cover.
Cook, stirring frequently, for 20 to 25 minutes.
Garnish as desired.

name: Super Easy Beef and Broccoli
directions: Heat oil in skillet.
Brown meat.
If using fresh onion and/or garlic, add to the meat while browning.
Boil rice and broccoli in water.
When meat is cooked, add gravy and soy (plus garlic powder and/or dried onion if used instead of fresh).
Heat through.

name: Lori B's Chili
directions: Brown hamburger and onion.
While browning hamburger, put the rest of the ingredients into a large pot.
Drain hamburger and add to pot.
Simmer on low heat for 30 minutes.
Can also be made in the crock pot.
I normally make this without the chili sauce because I have the other ingredients on hand.
Put into soup bowls, sprinkle with some cheese and enjoy.

name: Honey Mustard Marinade
directions: Mix all the ingredients together until they form a runny paste.
Score the chicken or chops and place to soak in the in marinade in tupperware or a covered bowl for at least 30 minutes.
Place the meat on a baking tray and pour any excess marinade over the top.
Place in the oven a bake until the meat is cooked to your taste.

name: Soul Seasoning Mix
directions: Blend spices together.
Store in spice jar or air tight container.
NOTE:  I add 2 teaspoons of this spice mixture to my beef taco meat mixture.

name: Steak Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Add Marinade over meat.
Let stand for 2 to 3 hours.
Remove from fridge 1 hour prior to grilling.

name: Bribe the Ranger Marinade
directions: Mix all ingredients in a ziploc bag.
Add meat and marinate 6-24 hours, refrigerated.
Grill or broil meat in the usual fashion.

name: Soy Sauce Marinade
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Pour over beef or pork and marinate 8 hours or overnight.
Grill or broil meat.
Leftover marinade should be discarded.

name: The Ultimate Spice Blend
directions: Mix ingredients in large bowl.

name: Easy, Spicy Beans and Rice
directions: In a 2 quart microwave-safe casserole, combine the first 6 ingredients.
Cover and cook on high for 5-7 minutes (until liquid is absorbed).
Stir in the beans, corn, tomatoes, and salsa.
Top with grated cheese.
Cover and microwave on high for 2-3 minutes more until heated through and cheese is melted.
Serve.

name: Finger Lickin' Good Chicken Marinade
directions: Place chicken in marinade (overnight if possible, turning once).

name: Maui Teriyaki Sauce Marinade
directions: Add first two ingredients into a large mixing bowl, stir until sugar is dissolved. Then add the remaining ingredients and stir well.  Pour over whatever meat you wish, beef, pork or chicken cover and marinate anywhere from 2 hours to 24 hours.

name: Seafood Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix dry ingredients together using a fork or place in a jar, cover and shake it thoroughly. Store in tightly covered container.

name: Lemon Marinade (Chicken)
directions: Mix everything together well.  Pour into a shallow dish or large zip top bag.
Add chicken.  If using a dish, cover.  Zip the bag if using a bag.  Stash in the refrigerator for at least 2 - 3 hours, turning occasionally.
Drain off the marinade and grill, or bake at 325 F for about an hour in a greased baking dish.

name: All-American Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.
Rub over steaks, pork or chicken; refrigerate up to 4 hours before grilling or broiling.

name: Simple Beef Marinade
directions: Combine ingredients and pour over your choice of beef in a large Ziploc bag. Work marinade into meat. Place bag in refrigerator. Turn every half-hour.
You can marinate as long as you like, there is no set time limit.
Works wonderfully with roasts, tenderloin, and steaks. Especially delicious when grilled!
